other_observers: Aaron Schippe , Jeannie Mitchell , Jackie Heyda , Mary Ellen Balog; Yes , I was the original finder on this day although Bill Maynard had glimpsed this bird a few days earlier.
specific_location: Chico Basin Ranch , Upper Twin Ponds , 50 yards south of dam wall , wet sedge meadow.
description: An Ammodramus sparrow flushed up from my feet in a wet sedge meadow and perched about 10 feet away briefly in a low bush before flying higher to a Russian Olive where it stayed for may be 30 seconds . Description is taken from photos . A flat headed sparrow with a faded orangy buff supercilium , gray auriculars , dark streaky lateral crown stripe , relatively stubby bill , broad buffy and dark back streaks , and short spiky tail. Was not able to get a frontal view on this bird .Behaviors: On flushing flew very low over sedge to small bush . Perched briefly in Russian Olive , mainly back views obtained with head turned around. Very nervous behaviour.Call: None heardPlumage: juvenile
photographer: Photographer: John Drummond, John Drummond, John Drummond, John Drummond, John Drummond
